purpose: Force skills heartbeats to produce reviewed, installable, discoverable skill value instead of unbounded catalog/status churn.
selection_rule: Operators must consume the highest-priority ready ticket allowed by gates.md. High-risk package-facing review outranks manual discovery fallback; discovery restoration outranks routine metadata cleanup.
definition_of_done: A done ticket must restore discovery, review a source/skill, normalize a skill, remove a risk blocker, repair catalog parity, publish/verify an installable surface, or kill unsafe/generic material. Status-only updates are not done.

tickets.mission: Restore deterministic discovery handoff production for public skill/source scouting or document the exact connector/runtime blocker and create the smallest repair path.
tickets.required_connector: GitHub/web only; no copying third-party content
acceptance_tests: explains why latest discovery handoff is absent
creates or queues a deterministic repair path
does not mirror third-party content
updates board and daily state with next source-review action

acceptance_result.evidence: reports/discovery/2026-07-08-discovery-handoff-repair.md documents that latest.json is absent while the producer workflow/scripts exist, queues manual fallback, and preserves risk/package gates.

tickets.supervisor_gate: Supervisor should keep publication/readiness blocked until deterministic discovery or a bounded manual fallback is working.
tickets.kill_condition: If connector cannot inspect workflow/action state, create a precise local-agent/CI repair ticket instead of pretending discovery is active.

tickets.mission: Review and safely wrap or redact the MCP tool-poisoning/SSRF skill so package-facing surfaces preserve defensive audit value without default executable probing detail.
acceptance_tests: classifies risk level and requires_review
removes default executable SSRF/probing detail if unsafe
preserves defensive audit framing
creates catalog-refresh ticket if package-facing metadata changed

acceptance_result.evidence: dist/skills/auditing-mcp-servers-for-tool-poisoning/SKILL.md now has risk_level high, requires_review true, authorization gate, and no package-facing default SSRF target list or executable probing snippets. reports/risk/2026-07-08-mcp-tool-poisoning-ssrf-risk-review.md records the review.

tickets.supervisor_gate: Routine metadata backlog and publication/package endorsement must remain blocked while catalog parity after this risk review is unresolved.
tickets.kill_condition: If the skill cannot be made safe as a defensive wrapper, remove from installable catalog or mark non-publishable.

tickets.mission: Run the connector-safe metadata-only manual discovery fallback because the canonical GitHub Action handoff is absent from the default branch.
acceptance_tests: records at least three real public candidate sources or exact searches/blockers
classifies each candidate as review_next, watch, or skip_or_low_priority
does not clone, execute, copy, import, normalize, publish, or package candidate content
creates review-next, watch, skip, risk, or source-review queue state

acceptance_result.evidence: reports/discovery/2026-07-08-manual.md, reports/discovery/2026-07-08-manual.json, and sources/candidates/2026-07-08-manual.json record four metadata-only public candidates: modelcontextprotocol/servers, vercel/ai, QuantaAlpha/GitTaskBench, and openclaw/openclaw. Three are review_next and one is watch. No clone, execution, copying, import, normalization, packaging, or publication occurred.

tickets.supervisor_gate: Manual fallback output is discovery metadata only and cannot be treated as normalized skill content.
tickets.kill_condition: If fewer than three real candidates can be found after explicit public searches, record exact searches and blockers; do not invent candidates.

tickets.mission: Review the metadata-only manual discovery candidates for license, usefulness, safety, runtime/security surface, and redistribution boundaries before any source profile, normalization, catalog, package, or publication work.
tickets.required_connector: public web/GitHub metadata only; no cloning or code execution
acceptance_tests: verifies license/redistribution boundary for each review_next candidate
classifies safety/runtime surface, including OpenClaw high-risk messaging/browser/cron/account surfaces
creates source profiles, skip/watch notes, risk items, or normalization queues only when justified
does not copy third-party content, clone repositories, execute code, publish, or package candidates

acceptance_result.evidence: reports/review/2026-07-08-manual-source-review.md and .json record license/risk/usefulness review. Source profiles were created for modelcontextprotocol/servers, vercel/ai, and openclaw/openclaw. GitTaskBench was left watch/license-blocked after LICENSE returned 404. OpenClaw got a high-risk source-runtime review queue. No clone, execution, implementation copy, normalization, package, or publication occurred.

tickets.supervisor_gate: No normalization, catalog endorsement, package endorsement, or publication until source review resolves license, usefulness, risk, security/runtime surface, and redistribution boundaries.
tickets.kill_condition: If license or runtime risk cannot be established, classify as watch or skip_or_low_priority rather than normalizing.

tickets.mission: Review the OpenClaw source profile's real messaging, account/channel, browser, cron, gateway, node, and host-tool surfaces and decide what, if anything, may be abstracted into package-facing Skills material.
acceptance_tests: classifies account/browser/cron/gateway/host-tool risk surfaces
defines allowed abstraction boundaries for any future OpenClaw-derived skill
blocks command snippets, account setup, browser automation, cron execution, and operational channel mutation detail unless explicitly safe
updates board and queues with either normalization, watch, or quarantine state

acceptance_result.evidence: reports/risk/2026-07-08-openclaw-source-runtime-surfaces-risk-review.md classifies all OpenClaw messaging/account/channel, browser/canvas/nodes/cron, gateway, host-tool, pairing, sandbox, daemon, and onboarding surfaces as high-risk; allows only generic original runtime-surface safety patterns; blocks commands, account/channel setup, browser automation, cron execution, gateway setup, daemon setup, and implementation content.

tickets.supervisor_gate: No OpenClaw-derived operational normalization or package-facing endorsement. Future OpenClaw-derived material may only be generic high-risk agent-runtime safety guidance with no commands, account/browser/cron/gateway/channel/setup detail, or copied implementation content.
tickets.kill_condition: If safe abstraction boundaries cannot be maintained, keep OpenClaw as source-profile/watch only and block normalization.

tickets.mission: Create a bounded original skill or review note from the reviewed Vercel AI SDK source profile, focused on provider abstraction and sandbox/tool safety, without copying third-party examples or implementation content.
acceptance_tests: writes original content only
attributes Vercel AI SDK as source metadata
does not copy README examples, code snippets, prompts, or implementation
classifies risk and requires_review based on tool/sandbox surface
creates catalog parity queue if dist skill content changes

acceptance_result.evidence: dist/skills/ai-sdk-provider-tool-safety/SKILL.md is original provider/tool/sandbox boundary guidance with source attribution, risk_level medium, requires_review true, and no copied third-party examples or implementation. reports/normalization/2026-07-08-vercel-ai-sdk-profile-normalization.md records the normalization and creates the catalog parity ticket.

tickets.supervisor_gate: Catalog/package/publication endorsement remains blocked until catalog parity after this normalization is verified.
tickets.kill_condition: If normalization would mostly copy or paraphrase examples, write a skip note instead.

tickets.mission: Verify or refresh catalog/install-manifest parity after adding the AI SDK Provider Tool Safety normalized skill.
acceptance_tests: catalog reflects the new AI SDK Provider Tool Safety skill and updated software-engineering/medium counts
install manifest remains coherent for installer selection fields
no npm publish attempted
board next ticket is updated

acceptance_result.evidence: reports/catalog/2026-07-08-ai-sdk-provider-tool-safety-catalog-parity.md verifies dist/catalog.json skill_count 1183, software-engineering 147, medium 434, software-engineering list entry ai-sdk-provider-tool-safety, coherent install-manifest selection fields, and no npm publish.

tickets.supervisor_gate: Catalog parity after the AI SDK Provider Tool Safety normalized skill is verified. Package/publication endorsement remains blocked by absent discovery Action handoff and open metadata backlog.
tickets.kill_condition: Block if generated surfaces cannot be updated or verified through the available connector; require local-agent/CI repair rather than hand-waving parity.

tickets.mission: Review Browser Trace before metadata/catalog endorsement because it attaches to browser/CDP sessions, captures screenshots and DOM, mentions Browserbase API key use, and includes local debugger/trace command snippets.
acceptance_tests: classifies browser/CDP/session/API-key/DOM/screenshot/filesystem trace surfaces
defines whether Browser Trace can remain package-facing as a read-only defensive debugging skill
redacts or gates command snippets and API-key/browser-session handling if needed
keeps catalog/package/publication endorsement blocked until review and catalog parity are complete

acceptance_result.evidence: dist/skills/browser-trace/SKILL.md now has risk_level high, requires_review true, review_gate authorized-read-only-browser-debugging-only, and no package-facing local debugger launch, capture, query, API-key, or remote-provider setup snippets. reports/risk/2026-07-08-browser-trace-risk-review.md records the review and keeps catalog/package/publication blocked until catalog parity.

tickets.supervisor_gate: Catalog/package/publication endorsement remains blocked until catalog parity after Browser Trace and metadata-backfill changes is verified.
tickets.kill_condition: If Browser Trace cannot be made safe as a review-gated read-only defensive debugging skill, quarantine it or mark non-publishable.

tickets.mission: Inspect GitHub Actions run/log state or execute the discovery scripts in a safe local/CI context to prove whether the producer fails in discovery, review, handoff write, commit, pull/rebase, or push.
tickets.required_connector: GitHub Actions logs or safe local/CI execution surface
acceptance_tests: identifies exact producer failure stage or verifies fresh handoff creation
does not clone or execute third-party candidate code
updates the producer workflow or scripts only if the failure is directly proven
keeps manual fallback available while the producer is unverified
tickets.supervisor_gate: Promote only when Action logs or safe local/CI execution are inspectable.
tickets.kill_condition: If the producer cannot be inspected by available tools, keep blocked and rely on manual metadata fallback.

tickets.mission: Regenerate or verify catalog/install-manifest parity after MCP/SSRF risk-review metadata and body changes.
acceptance_tests: catalog reflects changed risk/domain/review metadata
install manifest is coherent
no npm publish attempted
board next ticket is updated

acceptance_result.evidence: reports/catalog/2026-07-08-risk-review-catalog-parity.md verifies the MCP/tool-poisoning catalog entry and summary counts reflect security-defensive/high/requires_review metadata; install manifest selection fields remain coherent; no npm publish occurred.

tickets.supervisor_gate: Publication remains blocked until discovery and backlog gates are clean; catalog parity after the MCP/SSRF risk review is no longer the active blocker.
tickets.kill_condition: Block if generated surfaces cannot be updated via connector and require local script execution.

tickets.mission: Refresh or verify catalog/install-manifest parity after metadata backfill classified Autonomy Loop and Autoresearch Loop and risk review gated Browser Trace.
acceptance_tests: catalog reflects Autonomy Loop and Autoresearch Loop as agent-operations medium requires_review skills
catalog reflects Browser Trace as software-engineering high requires_review
catalog summary counts are updated or verified after generation
install manifest remains coherent
no npm publish attempted
tickets.supervisor_gate: Ready now that Browser Trace high-risk review is complete; package/publication endorsement remains blocked until catalog parity is verified and discovery Action handoff/backlog gates are clean.
tickets.kill_condition: If generated surfaces cannot be refreshed or verified through available tooling, create a local catalog build ticket.

tickets.mission: Process one bounded batch from the uncategorized/unspecified-risk backlog, stopping immediately on any high-risk executable, credential, offensive, account, or external-mutation surface.
acceptance_tests: processes a bounded batch, not the entire catalog
adds domain/risk/requires_review/source status when justified
stops and creates risk ticket on unsafe material
creates catalog refresh ticket after metadata changes

acceptance_result.evidence: reports/critic/2026-07-08-metadata-backfill-batch.md documents a bounded batch: classified autonomy-loop and autoresearch-loop as agent-operations/medium/requires_review with source status; stopped at browser-trace due browser/CDP/session/API-key/DOM/screenshot trace surfaces; opened skills-risk-review-browser-trace-001 and a blocked catalog parity ticket after metadata changes.

tickets.supervisor_gate: Further metadata backlog cleanup is blocked until Browser Trace high-risk review and metadata-backfill catalog parity are complete.
tickets.kill_condition: Stop if the batch becomes generic metadata churn with no package-facing value.
